current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.

recent_results.description: Run a gated K=128 proxy stabilization pass at `LR=2e-4` on the current EF=16 signum baseline to test whether a lower learning rate improves FVU on the main quality anchor.
Run a gated K=128 proxy stabilization pass with `EXPANSION_FACTOR=4` on the current signum baseline, keeping LR, AUXK, and architecture fixed.
Fix the `factorized_topk` implementation so it passes sanity, then rerun the same K=128 proxy prototype under the current strongest signum regime.

recent_round_summaries.hypothesis: The lower-LR gated sweep was a clean regression, so the next narrow exploitation move is to test the remaining untried capacity point on the best stable family. Reducing gated `EXPANSION_FACTOR` from 16 to 4 at K=128 may reveal a non-dominated quality-cost tradeoff and will close the last obvious one-parameter sweep on the current anchor before returning to broader architecture work.

The obvious gated one-parameter sweep is now exhausted after clean regressions on both lower LR and lower expansion, so the next least-bad informative move is to unblock the incubating `factorized_topk` family rather than introduce another fresh direction. Its only evidence so far is a `sanity_failed` prototype, which should be treated as an architecture-compatibility issue, not a quality result. A narrow code fix inside `sparsify/` to make `factorized_topk` forward/backward compatible with the runtime should recover one valid proxy measurement at K=128 under the existing strongest regime.

The straightforward gated hyperparameter sweep has now produced only clean regressions, so the next informative move is to recover the blocked incubating family rather than spend another round on weak local tuning. `factorized_topk` still has no usable quality signal because its only attempt failed at sanity. A narrow architecture-compatibility fix inside `sparsify/` should let the same K=128 proxy prototype run and finally reveal whether extra encoder mixing can improve over plain top-k.

factorized_topk.design_hypothesis: The straightforward gated hyperparameter sweep has now produced only clean regressions, so the next informative move is to recover the blocked incubating family rather than spend another round on weak local tuning. `factorized_topk` still has no usable quality signal because its only attempt failed at sanity. A narrow architecture-compatibility fix inside `sparsify/` should let the same K=128 proxy prototype run and finally reveal whether extra encoder mixing can improve over plain top-k.
next_steps: If the repaired `factorized_topk` run completes and beats plain `topk`, keep the family alive and stabilize its internal factorization width or activation design next.
If the repaired run completes normally but remains far behind gated, retire shallow factorization and move to a different encoder-expressivity direction.
If `factorized_topk` still fails sanity after the focused fix, conclude that this prototype path is not presently viable in the stack and shift effort elsewhere.

recent_performance_findings: round 39: `group_topk` still has no valid quality signal because its first actual proxy run timed out at the first step, which is stronger evidence of an implementation bottleneck than of a bad architecture. The next coherent move is a performance-focused code pass that makes the `group_topk` encoder path more execution-friendly so the family can receive one clean proxy measurement at K=128 under the strongest current regime. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=None, k=None, health=crash, termination=sanity_failed)

round 41: The recent `first_step_timeout` crashes are not specific to `group_topk`: the gated AUXK=0 recovery run also advanced through the first few microbatches and then stalled at the first optimizer step. That points to a shared training-path bottleneck rather than an architecture verdict. Replacing CUDA's per-step fired-latent `cat`+advanced-index update with an incremental boolean fired mask in `sparsify/trainer.py` should remove that first-step spike and let the unresolved `group_topk` K=128 proxy run complete under the current strongest regime. -> crash (fvu=None, observed_fvu=None, k=None, health=crash, termination=sanity_failed)
